BASE.name: ATTACK_VECTOR
BASE.justification: Unescaped addError messages can be triggered via web requests or user-controlled inputs and rendered in the UI.

BASE.name: ATTACK_COMPLEXITY
BASE.justification: Straightforward XSS-style injection is possible if attacker-controlled content is rendered without encoding.

BASE.name: PRIVILEGES_REQUIRED
BASE.justification: The issue may be exploitable without authentication depending on the entry point.

BASE.name: USER_INTERACTION
BASE.justification: A user must view the page where the unescaped error message is displayed.

BASE.name: SCOPE
BASE.justification: The impact remains within the same application and browser execution context.

BASE.name: CONFIDENTIALITY
BASE.justification: Limited exposure is possible through access to page context or session-related information.

BASE.name: INTEGRITY
BASE.justification: Client-side content or behavior may be manipulated without affecting backend data.

BASE.name: AVAILABILITY
BASE.justification: The issue does not impact application availability.

TEMPORAL.name: EXPLOIT_CODE_MATURITY
TEMPORAL.justification: XSS payloads targeting unescaped output are well known and reliably exploitable.

TEMPORAL.name: REMEDIATION_LEVEL
TEMPORAL.justification: Enabling proper output encoding or escaping fully mitigates the vulnerability.

TEMPORAL.name: REPORT_CONFIDENCE
TEMPORAL.justification: The rule accurately detects unescaped output patterns such as escaping=false.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_ATTACK_VECTOR
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: The vulnerable page or component is accessed remotely over HTTP(S), allowing exploitation via crafted requests.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_ATTACK_COMPLEXITY
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: No additional environmental protections significantly increase the difficulty of exploiting unescaped output.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_PRIVILEGES_REQUIRED
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: Attackers do not require authentication or elevated privileges if the affected page is reachable.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_USER_INTERACTION
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: A user must view the affected page for the unescaped content to be rendered and executed.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_SCOPE
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: The impact does not extend beyond the current application and browser security boundary.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_CONFIDENTIALITY
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: Unescaped error output may expose limited client-side information but not direct backend data.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_INTEGRITY
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: Injected content can alter UI behavior or presentation without modifying server-side state.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_AVAILABILITY
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: The vulnerability does not affect service uptime or performance.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: CONFIDENTIALITY_REQUIREMENT
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: Protecting UI-rendered data and session context is important but not typically mission-critical.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: INTEGRITY_REQUIREMENT
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: Maintaining correct UI behavior and preventing client-side manipulation is important for user trust.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: AVAILABILITY_REQUIREMENT
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: Availability is not a primary concern for this client-side output-escaping issue.
